what_it_does: Parses JSONL conversation archives, auto-detecting whether they use the Claude or Codex harness, and emits normalized ConversationExchange records with user/assistant text, metadata, and tool calls.
observations: Implements harness auto-detection by inspecting the first non-empty JSON line for Codex-specific payload types.
Contains two large, nearly parallel parser implementations: parseClaudeConversation and parseCodexConversation.
Builds exchanges incrementally by streaming lines with readline and finalizing when a new user message appears or the file ends.
Generates exchange IDs with an MD5 hash over archivePath plus line range.
Extracts text from string or array-of-blocks message content.
Captures tool_use blocks in Claude and several Codex payload types (function_call, custom_tool_call, tool_search_call, local_shell_call).
Associates Codex tool results by call_id but leaves Claude tool_result matching as a TODO.
Derives project name from cwd when available, falling back to the parent directory of the file path.
Propagates session/turn metadata in Codex from session_meta and turn_context records.
standouts: Dual-harness normalization from two different JSONL schemas into a single ConversationExchange type.
Inner finalizeExchange/startExchange/appendToolCall/appendToolResult closures make the Codex parser state-machine style.
Tool-call result association is implemented for Codex but stubbed for Claude.
conventions: Uses async generator readline loops for streaming JSONL parsing.
Prefers crypto.createHash('md5') for deterministic local IDs.
Uses crypto.randomUUID() for tool-call IDs when no native call_id exists.
Represents optional arrays as undefined when empty rather than empty arrays.
Closes over mutable state (currentExchange, lineNumber, maps) rather than functional reduction.
Type interfaces mirror the expected JSONL schema fields.
concerns: Two distinct inner functions named finalizeExchange in different scopes may confuse symbol navigation.
Claude tool_result blocks are noted but not matched to their originating tool_use, leaving tool results incomplete.
MD5 exchange IDs could collide across different archives with overlapping line ranges.
parseConversationFile splits the path on '/' instead of using path.dirname/path.basename, making it platform-fragile.
Codex parser does not propagate parentUuid or isSidechain even if payload contains them.
Claude parser sets both claudeVersion and agentVersion to the same parsed.version value, losing distinction.
Heavy use of any in Codex payload handling reduces type safety.
notes: This is the ingestion/normalization layer that turns raw Claude/Codex JSONL logs into structured exchange records.
questions: What is the expected schema for thinkingMetadata.triggers?
Are Codex payload types exhaustive or should more response_item subtypes be handled?
How should Claude tool_result blocks be matched when multiple tool_use calls occur in one exchange?
Is the project name fallback in parseConversationFile intended to handle nested archive directories?
What determines whether a message with only tool results should start a new exchange?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to parse and normalize conversation archives; it does so for both Claude and Codex schemas, though with incomplete tool-result association for Claude, so purpose and actual largely match with noted gaps.
apparent_themes: Lossy but practical extraction of conversational structure.
Metadata provenance tracking (session, cwd, git branch, model).
intention_signals: It tries to provide a single, uniform view of heterogeneous AI-agent conversation logs so downstream indexing/recollection can treat Claude and Codex archives interchangeably.
novelty: The dual-harness auto-detection and normalization of both Anthropic-style message JSONL and OpenAI Codex rollout JSONL into one exchange model is the distinctive feature.
contribution: A concrete, file-local implementation of conversation-log ingestion that defines how two different harness schemas map to the shared ConversationExchange shape, including text extraction, metadata propagation, and tool-call capture.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript JSONL parser that auto-detects Claude or Codex conversation formats and normalizes them into structured ConversationExchange records with messages, metadata, and tool calls.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a unified ingestion path for heterogeneous AI conversation archives so they can be stored, searched, and recalled as normalized exchanges.

symbols.does: Describes the expected shape of a Claude-style JSONL log line.
symbols.description: Defines fields for message type, role, content, timestamp, uuid, parentUuid, isSidechain, session/cwd/git/version metadata, and thinking metadata.

symbols.does: Describes the expected shape of a Codex rollout JSONL log line.
symbols.description: Contains timestamp, type, and an untyped payload used to dispatch session metadata, turn context, and response items.

symbols.does: Mutable accumulator used while scanning lines to build a single conversation exchange.
symbols.description: Holds the user message, assistant message fragments, line positions, metadata, and tool calls until the exchange is finalized.

symbols.does: Reads the first non-empty JSON line of a file to decide whether it is a Codex or Claude archive.
symbols.description: Returns 'codex' if the line contains a payload with recognized Codex event types; otherwise returns 'claude'.

symbols.does: Routes a file to the Claude or Codex parser based on detected harness.
symbols.description: Calls detectConversationHarness and then invokes parseClaudeConversation or parseCodexConversation with the supplied project name and archive path.

symbols.does: Parses a Claude JSONL archive into ConversationExchange records.
symbols.description: Streams lines, finalizes the previous exchange on each user message, accumulates assistant text and tool_use blocks, and propagates metadata such as session, cwd, git branch, model, and thinking settings.

symbols.does: Closes the current Claude exchange and pushes it to the result array.
symbols.description: Computes an MD5 id from archivePath and line range, assigns the id to tool calls, joins assistant messages, and emits a ConversationExchange if any assistant output exists.

symbols.does: Pulls plain text out of a string or array of content blocks.
symbols.description: Returns the string directly when content is a string, otherwise filters object blocks that have a text property and joins their texts with newlines.

symbols.does: Attempts to parse a string as JSON, falling back to the original string.
symbols.description: Used mainly to convert Codex tool argument strings into structured objects when possible.
